page.content: In the context of assessing the rigor and reliability of the survey instrument used to evaluate snoring behavior, the data presented in the accompanying table offers critical insights into various psychometric properties. The metrics of Language Clarity and Relevance are particularly important as they highlight the effectiveness of the survey’s wording and its alignment with the study's objectives. A total of 58 participants strongly agreed on the clarity of the language used, which suggests that the questions were well-understood, thereby enhancing the quality of the responses collected. Furthermore, the table includes the Cronbach’s alpha coefficient, reported at 0.89 (95% CI: 0.85-0.92), indicating a high level of internal consistency among the items related to snoring behavior. This metric is essential for confirming that the questions are measuring the same underlying construct. Additionally, the inclusion of the Kappa statistic reinforces the reliability of the responses, providing reassurance that the data collected is both reliable and valid. The combination of these metrics not only underscores the robustness of the survey tool but also its potential applicability in future research endeavors focused on sleep-related issues.
